name: Cordillera Subbética – trail running through the Sierra and Summit of La Sagra
name_original: Cordillera Subbética – trail running por la Sierra y Cima de la Sagra
region_intro: Jogging in Sierras Del Nordeste offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes. The region features a mix of challenging ascents and descents, with routes often traversing through natural parks and rugged areas. Many trails provide opportunities to experience the varied topography of the Sierras, from high peaks to valleys. Best jogging routes in Sierras Del Nordeste The most popular jogging route is Cordillera Subbética – trail running through the Sierra and Summit of La Sagra, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico de la Sagra Summit loop from Observatorio astronómico de La Sagra, a difficult 8.9 miles (14.4 km) path. This running route ascends to a prominent summit, offering expansive views of the surrounding Sierras. Local runners also love the Muerto – Cerro Atalaya loop from Huéscar, a 14.0 miles (22.6 km) trail leading through varied mountain landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Jogging in Sierras Del Nordeste is defined by mountainous ascents, challenging descents, and panoramic views, with options for advanced jogging trails. The network offers routes for different ability levels, including some moderate and difficult running routes. The routes in Sierras Del Nordeste are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 3 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Sierras Del Nordeste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: There are over 35 dedicated running routes in Sierras Del Nordeste, catering to various skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 13 moderate routes, and 21 difficult routes, offering a wide range of options for runners.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: While Sierras Del Nordeste is known for its challenging mountainous terrain, there is at least one easy running route available. For those looking for a less strenuous option, consider exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cortijo del Cigarral&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.1 km path with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of distances can I expect from the running trails in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: Running trails in Sierras Del Nordeste vary significantly in distance. You can find shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cortijo del Cigarral&lt;/a&gt; at 5.1 km, to much longer and more challenging routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua loop from La Matea&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 46 km.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sierras Del Nordeste are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31904230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Sagra Summit loop from Observatorio astronómico de La Sagra&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muerto – Cerro Atalaya loop from Huéscar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while running in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: The region offers several impressive natural and historical landmarks. Runners might encounter sights like the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sagra Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the unique rock formation of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1277387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Toro&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Martyrs of the Mountain&lt;/a&gt;. These highlights often provide stunning views and points of interest along the trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: The running trails in Sierras Del Nordeste are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 out of 5 stars. Runners often praise the challenging mountainous ascents, panoramic views, and the diverse terrain that defines the region's routes.

Q: Are there advanced or challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. Sierras Del Nordeste is particularly well-suited for advanced runners, with 21 out of 35 routes classified as difficult. These trails feature significant elevation gains and rugged terrain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986576312/cordillera-subbetica-trail-running-through-the-sierra-and-summit-of-la-sagra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cordillera Subbética – trail running through the Sierra and Summit of La Sagra&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial ascents.

Q: Is public transport available to access jogging routes in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: Access to specific trailheads in Sierras Del Nordeste via public transport can be limited due to the mountainous and rural nature of the region. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for towns like Huéscar or La Matea, but for many routes, personal vehicle access is often more convenient.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: The best time for running in Sierras Del Nordeste generally depends on the specific route and your tolerance for heat or cold. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures, which are ideal for tackling the region's significant elevation changes. Summers can be very hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winters may bring snow to higher peaks, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer opportunities for wildlife spotting?
A: Given that many routes traverse through natural parks and rugged areas, there are opportunities for wildlife spotting, particularly in quieter, less frequented sections. Keep an eye out for local bird species and other mountain fauna, especially during dawn or dusk.

Q: Are there any hidden gem running trails in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: While popular routes like those around La Sagra are well-known, exploring some of the less-trafficked moderate or difficult trails can lead to discovering hidden gems. These often offer equally stunning views and a more secluded running experience away from the main tourist paths.

Q: What should I bring for a run on the trails of Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: Due to the mountainous terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, it's recommended to bring appropriate trail running shoes, sufficient water, sun protection, and layers of clothing. For longer or more remote routes, consider carrying a map or GPS device, and emergency supplies.
tips: Top of the hill of the same name and highest point of the Altiplano Granadino and the Subbética mountain range, with 2383 meters above sea level. Its prominence (1,408 m) is striking, making it one of the most prominent peaks in the Iberian Peninsula and therefore demanding ascent as it has steep slopes. These slopes are largely covered with pine from Salta.
